name: Bunny.net API
what_it_does: Bunny.net — affordable CDN and edge storage platform with REST API for managing pull zones, storage zones, video libraries, and image optimization at globally distributed edge locations.
use_cases: Agents managing CDN cache purging after content updates to ensure users see fresh content immediately
Storage management — agents uploading, organizing, and deleting files in Bunny Storage via REST API
Video delivery — agents uploading and managing video content in Bunny Stream for agent-generated video outputs
Image optimization — agents serving user uploads through Bunny's image processing API for automatic resizing and format conversion
Edge caching configuration — agents creating and configuring pull zones for global asset distribution
not_for: Teams requiring enterprise SLAs with dedicated support — Bunny is SMB/developer focused
Complex serverless edge compute — use Cloudflare Workers for programmable edge logic
Very high-volume video transcoding — use specialized video platforms for production video pipelines
best_when: You need cost-effective global CDN and edge storage with simple REST API management — Bunny's pricing is significantly cheaper than Cloudflare or AWS CloudFront.
avoid_when: You need edge compute, programmable request handling, or enterprise-grade SLAs — Cloudflare or Fastly are better choices.

auth.notes: API key from Bunny.net dashboard. Storage zone-specific passwords for storage API. Account API key for management. Keys passed in AccessKey header.

pricing.paid_tiers: Bandwidth: from $0.005/GB (cheapest EU); Storage: $0.02/GB/month; Video encoding: $0.015/minute
pricing.notes: Among the cheapest CDN providers — significantly lower than Cloudflare or AWS. Volume discounts available. No free tier but extremely cost-effective at all scales.

performance.rate_limits: Management API: 100 req/s; storage API: high throughput; CDN: no limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documentation is comprehensive. Error responses include status codes and messages. Cache purge confirmation is synchronous. Storage API uses different base URL than management API.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Cache purge is idempotent (purging already-cleared cache is a no-op). File uploads overwrite existing files (idempotent). Pull zone creation is not idempotent — creates duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: Storage API uses storage.bunnycdn.com base URL, not api.bunny.net — easy to confuse the two endpoints
Storage zone passwords are different from account API key — wrong credential gives 401 on storage operations
Cache purge is eventually consistent globally — purge call returns success but edge nodes may serve cached content for seconds after
Pull zone and storage zone are separate concepts — agents must create both and link them for CDN-backed storage
Video Library API is a separate product (Bunny Stream) with different pricing and auth from core storage

security_score_components.security_notes: GDPR compliant. TLS enforced. Storage zone-specific passwords add some isolation. Account-level API key has no scope granularity. No SOC2 documented.
